title: De-escalation of adjuvant radio(chemo)therapy for patients with HPV-positive head and neck squamous cell carcinoma: study protocol for a phase I trial to reduce late toxicity (DELPHI)
author: Linge, Annett

steps.title: The interventional multicentric DELPHI trial is...
steps.instruction: The interventional multicentric DELPHI trial is investigating step-wise radiation dose reduction in patients with both p16-overexpressing and HPV16 DNA positive OPSCC. Depending on international clinical and histopathological risk factors, patients are being enrolled in the high-risk or intermediate-risk arm. Patients of the high-risk arm are receiving standard simultaneous chemotherapy with cisplatin. Patients with smoking history of at least 30 packyears are being treated in the observational arm.

steps.title: Primary endpoint of the DELPHI trial...
steps.instruction: Primary endpoint of the DELPHI trial is LRC after 24 months. Secondary endpoints are acute and late toxicity, quality of life during and up to 24 months after the end of therapy as well as LRC and overall survival after 60 months.
